ai_insights.dashboard: Your best performing content this week was a quick color grading tutorial that hit 60% average retention — significantly above your channel average. Short-form tutorials with a clear outcome in the title are driving the most discovery. Focus on replicating that hook-and-payoff structure across platforms.
ai_insights.title:
• Titles with a specific time promise ("in 5 minutes") outperform vague titles by 3x
• "How I" and "POV:" framing consistently drives higher click-through on TikTok
• Titles with numbers ("3 Mistakes", "5 lens attachments") perform well on YouTube but poorly on TikTok
• Your lowest performers have generic titles with no clear audience or outcome

reasons: 60.5% average retention is well above YouTube's average of ~40%, signaling the tutorial format kept viewers engaged throughout
4.2% CTR indicates the thumbnail and title combination resonated strongly — the specific time promise attracted clicks
84 subscribers gained shows the content converted casual viewers into followers, a sign the value was clear and credible
8.5% engagement rate is exceptional — the relatable POV format invites comments and shares from creators who feel seen
410 shares suggest the content was forwarded to others as useful or entertaining, dramatically expanding organic reach
Short 28-second length aligns with TikTok's retention algorithm, likely resulting in broad distribution
1.1% CTR is below average — the title 'mistakes every new videographer makes' is too broad and doesn't promise a specific outcome
Only 4 subscribers gained despite decent retention suggests the audience was interested but not compelled enough to follow
Low view count (890) combined with decent retention (52.8%) means the bottleneck is discovery, not content quality
1,200 views is low for TikTok — the tutorial format works better as YouTube long-form; TikTok rewards emotion and entertainment
The 45-second length is in a performance dead zone — too long for casual scrollers, too short to fully teach a technique
Low share count (8) means the content wasn't passed along — adding a clear 'try this yourself' moment can fix this

trends.title: From Chaos to Cinematic: The 'Raw vs Edit' Reveal
trends.what_it_is: Creators show raw, unedited footage side-by-side with the final polished version — often with a dramatic music drop at the reveal moment. This format works across YouTube long-form, Reels, and TikTok. It makes editing feel like magic and positions the creator as skilled without being preachy.
trends.why_it_works: The contrast is immediately satisfying to watch — it triggers the same dopamine response as before-and-after content in any niche. It also demonstrates expertise without explanation, which builds trust faster than tutorials. The reveal moment is inherently shareable.
how_to_implement: Film your next project and save 30–60 seconds of raw, ungraded footage from a challenging scene
Edit the final version normally, then cut together a split-screen or sequential comparison
Add a dramatic music build that drops exactly at the reveal — this is the key moment
Post as a Reel/TikTok with the caption 'What I started with vs what I delivered' and let the contrast do the work

trends.title: The '60-Second Client Film' Challenge
trends.what_it_is: Creators condense an entire event or project — a wedding, corporate shoot, real estate walkthrough — into exactly 60 seconds with music and tight cuts. The constraint forces quality decisions and the result is both a portfolio piece and a highly watchable social clip.
trends.why_it_works: It serves two audiences at once: potential clients who see finished work, and creator peers who want to study the editing style. The 60-second format is optimized for Instagram Reels and TikTok autoplay, so it gets pushed to new audiences organically. It also positions you as efficient and professional.
how_to_implement: Take your most recent completed project and pick only the 8–10 best shots — ruthlessly cut the rest
Choose a licensed track with a clear 60-second structure (intro, build, drop) from Epidemic Sound or Artlist
Sync cuts to the music beats and hit exactly 58–62 seconds total
Add your logo for 2 seconds at the end and post with location tags and client-industry hashtags
